parameters.subject: =LinkedIn connections: identify run — {{ $json.targets.length }} targets
parameters.message:
={{ $json.summary + '

Tier counts: ' + JSON.stringify($json.tier_counts) + ( $json.parse_error ? ('
Parse issue: ' + $json.parse_error) : '' ) + '

--- Notion schema (LinkedIn Connection Queue) ---
Name (Title), Title (Text), Company (Text), Tier (Select 1|2|3), Sector (Text), LinkedInURL (URL), Status (Select: Pending | Note Ready | Connected | Follow-up 1 Sent | Follow-up 2 Sent | Follow-up 3 Sent | Closed), ConnectionNote (Text), FollowUp1-3 (Text), IdentifiedAt, ConnectedAt, PublishedAt, Signal (Text).

Pages are being created in Notion for each target (see database). Review before any outreach. Nothing is auto-sent to LinkedIn.' }}

parameters.responseBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ ok: true, mode: 'identify', count: ($('Code: Parse Identify Targets').first().json.targets || []).length, tier_counts: $('Code: Parse Identify Targets').first().json.tier_counts }) }}

parameters.jsCode:
const { targets, sector } = $input.first().json;
return (targets || []).map((t) => ({ json: { ...t, sector_fallback: sector } }));

nodes.name: Notion: Create Connection Row
nodes.notes: Map property keys to your Notion database. Title property for person name is the page title; column Title is job title.

parameters.subject: =LinkedIn connection note — {{ $json.email_name }} (review before sending)
parameters.message:
={{ ($json.tier3_skip ? 'Tier 3: send connection with NO note (leave message blank).

' : '') + 'Tier: ' + $json.email_tier + '

Note (manual paste only; not auto-sent):
' + $json.connection_note + ($json.ambiguous ? '

(Two variants in note — pick one.)' : '') }}

parameters.responseBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ ok: true, mode: 'connect', notion_page_id: $('Code: Prepare Connect Email').first().json.notion_page_id, ambiguous: $('Code: Prepare Connect Email').first().json.ambiguous, tier3_skip: $('Code: Prepare Connect Email').first().json.tier3_skip }) }}

nodes.name: HTTP: Notion Patch Follow-up
nodes.notes: Property names FollowUp1, FollowUp2, FollowUp3, Status must match your LinkedIn Connection Queue database exactly.

parameters.subject: =LinkedIn follow-up {{ $('Code: Build Followup Notion Patch').first().json.followup_number }} draft — {{ $('Code: Build Followup Notion Patch').first().json.name }}

parameters.responseBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ ok: true, mode: 'followup', followup_number: $('Code: Build Followup Notion Patch').first().json.followup_number }) }}
